description:
You are acting as an MLOps engineer who is tracking which binary artifacts are produced most often during a set of machine-learning experiments.

    1. In the directory /home/user/experiments you will find two plain-text log files:
       • /home/user/experiments/log1.txt  
       • /home/user/experiments/log2.txt  

       Each line of these logs contains exactly one file name of an experiment artifact (e.g., “model_v1.ckpt”, “metrics_v1.json”, etc.).  
       No blank lines or extra whitespace appear in the logs.

    2. Your task is to create a summary file called /home/user/experiments/artifact_frequency.txt that counts how many times each unique artifact appears across BOTH logs.

    3. Output format requirements (must be followed exactly so that automated tests can verify the file):
       • Each line in artifact_frequency.txt must contain two fields separated by a single space:  
         <count><space><artifact_file_name>
       • <count> must be the integer frequency of the artifact across log1.txt + log2.txt.  
       • Lines must be sorted first by count in DESCENDING numerical order, and for counts that are the same, by the artifact file name in ASCENDING (alphabetical) order.  
       • Do NOT include header rows or extra spaces.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline character.

    4. Save artifact_frequency.txt in /home/user/experiments with the exact name and path shown above.

    5. Only standard command-line utilities available in a base Ubuntu image may be used; the canonical solution can be achieved with some combination of sort and uniq (and optionally cut, cat, etc.).  You do NOT need root privileges.

    6. When you are done, you may print the contents of /home/user/experiments/artifact_frequency.txt to the console to show your result, but the presence and correctness of the file itself is what the automatic grader will check.

    The directory is writable, and you have full read/write permissions for the logs and for the file you must create.
truth:
Initial files that already exist before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/experiments/log1.txt  
       Contents (exact, with trailing newline):  
       model_v1.ckpt  
       dataset_v1.zip  
       model_v1.ckpt  
       metrics_v1.json  
       dataset_v1.zip  
       ⏎

    2. /home/user/experiments/log2.txt  
       Contents (exact, with trailing newline):  
       model_v2.ckpt  
       dataset_v1.zip  
       model_v1.ckpt  
       metrics_v1.json  
       metrics_v1.json  
       model_v2.ckpt  
       ⏎

    Expected file that must be created by the agent:

    3. /home/user/experiments/artifact_frequency.txt  
       Required contents (exact, with trailing newline):  
       4 model_v1.ckpt  
       3 dataset_v1.zip  
       3 metrics_v1.json  
       2 model_v2.ckpt  
       ⏎
